mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate compresses equity discount rates and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.0% signals a tight labor market that squeezes corporate profit margins through wage pressures, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of unemployment below 4.0% and a declining federal funds rate accelerates household income growth and consumption, producing strongly positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is non-decomposable because low unemployment without monetary accommodation does not generate equivalent returns.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE is below 4.0% and FEDFUNDS decreased over the prior 3 months, SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or single-series effects explain the return.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a contraction in retail sales reflects consumer exhaustion and an impending demand pullback, causing rapid negative S&P 500 repricing over the next week.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with elevated consumer leverage
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS growth episodes in late-cycle regimes fail to produce negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects capacity utilization recovery and operating leverage expansion, lifting corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between INDPRO changes and 60-day SPX returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,300 thousand units alongside positive GDP growth confirm durable demand momentum in interest-rate-sensitive sectors, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as cyclical earnings estimates revise upward.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1,300 and GDPC1 is expanding, 5-day SPX returns are negative in the majority of observations.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of accelerating CPI inflation and a stagnant federal funds rate signals monetary policy falling behind the curve, eroding real equity returns and triggering a 60-day drawdown as forward earnings multiples contract.
regime_hint: monetary policy divergence from inflation trend
failure_condition: Joint periods of rising CPIAUCSL and flat or falling FEDFUNDS do not produce negative 60-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth directly lifts aggregate wage income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through earnings expansion.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS surprises fail to correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns, producing a Rank IC below 0.03.
